title: Licania platycalyx
taxonomy: Licania platycalyx belongs to the family of plants known as Chrysobalanaceae. This family is a part of the larger order of plants known as Rosales. The species Licania platycalyx is native to Central and South America. Licania platycalyx is classified as a type of flowering plant, specifically a shrub or small tree. The classification of Licania platycalyx reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within its family and order.

intro: Licania platycalyx, also known as the Brazilian cherry or Amazonian cherry, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Chrysobalanaceae.
